title: Java Primitive Types
description: Practice primitive declarations and numeric suffixes.
learningGoals: Practice boolean and numeric literal syntax.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the literal 1_500L assign to timeoutMs?
choices: The int value 1500, which then overflows because of the L suffix
The long value 1500; the underscore is ignored and L marks it as long
Compilation fails because underscores are not allowed inside numeric literals
completionChecks.explanation: Underscores between digits are readability separators that the compiler ignores, and the L suffix makes the literal a long, so timeoutMs holds 1500.

completionChecks.prompt: What does enabled hold after boolean enabled = retries > 0; when retries is 3?
choices: Compilation fails because a boolean cannot be assigned a comparison result
completionChecks.explanation: The relational expression retries > 0 evaluates to the boolean true when retries is 3, and that boolean value is assigned to enabled.

completionChecks.prompt: What does this print if System.out.println(grade + ":" + ratio); is added after the snippet?
choices: Compilation fails because char cannot be concatenated with double
completionChecks.explanation: The colon makes the expression a String concatenation before ratio is appended, so the char prints as A instead of its numeric code.
